creator: swhetzel@gmail.com
text:
On Sun, Feb 3, 2008 at 2:05 PM, ross <rostyslav@optonline.net> wrote:
>
> >Number:         120243
> >Category:       amd64
> >Synopsis:       can't get work  Broadcom 802.11b/g WLAN on HP Pavilion DV8000 with DeskTopBSD 1.6

> the system shows interface -Broadcom 802.11b/g WLAN- as -fwe0-, but it does not work at all. Help me please.
> i turned on in loader.conf "if_fwe_load="YES" and in KERNEL device fwe, device firewire is uncomment. i don't know what to do else.

To get the wireless working on the dv8000 systems, you need to
download the Windows XP driver:

4.40.19.0. - ftp://ftp.hp.com/pub/softpaq/sp33001-33500/sp33008.exe

extract the files using archivers/cabextract, then use ndisgen to
build the ndis driver for your wireless adapter.

Scot
